headline: Sudan floor sustaining with new conduct documentation. UN Fact-Finding Mission (Feb 2026) confirmed RSF genocidal campaign in El-Fasher: 6,000+ documented killings in 3 days post-capture, with three Genocide Convention prohibited acts confirmed. Al Jazeera (Apr 27): thousands held by RSF in El-Fasher. 33.7M humanitarian aid need; 28.9M acutely food insecure; 65%+ hospitals closed; appeal 40% funded. Floor designation (April 30 2026) maintained with formal genocide-finding new institutional framing. Composite 0 sustained. Recommend confirm with floor-cluster new-conduct documentation.
key_evidence: OHCHR / UN Fact-Finding Mission Feb 2026: El-Fasher evidence reveals genocidal campaign by RSF — three Genocide Convention prohibited acts confirmed; 6,000+ killings in 3 days post-capture, actual toll significantly higher.
Al Jazeera April 27 2026: thousands reportedly held by RSF in El-Fasher.
33.7M people in humanitarian need; 28.9M acutely food insecure; 65%+ hospitals closed.
International humanitarian appeal at 40% funded — sustained funding gap.
HRW World Report 2026 (Sudan): comprehensive 2025 SAF-RSF civil war documentation.
Floor designation in countries.json formalized April 30 2026; this cycle adds genocide-finding institutional framing as new conduct documentation.

evidence.source: OHCHR Feb 2026 — Sudan El-Fasher genocidal campaign
evidence.finding: UN Fact-Finding Mission confirms RSF genocidal campaign targeting non-Arab populations; three Genocide Convention prohibited acts; 6,000+ killings in 3 days.
Thousands held by RSF in El-Fasher; ongoing detention pattern.
SAF-RSF civil war: ethnic cleansing patterns, sexual violence, attacks on civilians, humanitarian access denial.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1 floor sustained. State capacity to recognize civilian suffering effectively destroyed by SAF-RSF dual-belligerent dynamics.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1 floor sustained. Both belligerent parties demonstrate sustained absence of empathic regard for civilian populations.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1 floor sustained. Active obstruction of humanitarian access by both parties; appeal 40% funded.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1 floor sustained. UN-confirmed targeting of non-Arab populations meets Genocide Convention threshold for prohibited acts; 3+ children/day dying El-Fasher (carried from prior cycle).
scoring_rationale.BND: 1 floor sustained. Both parties cross fundamental boundaries on civilian protection, IHL, and humanitarian-corridor obligations; thousands held by RSF.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1 floor sustained. No functioning state accountability mechanism; both parties refuse jurisdiction over conduct.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1 floor sustained. State system dissolved across most of country; war-economy entrenchment.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1 floor sustained. Both parties' declared commitments contradicted by sustained genocidal-pattern conduct.

floor_status.new_conduct_this_cycle: UN Fact-Finding Mission Feb 2026 formal genocide-finding for El-Fasher campaign — institutional framing added to floor rationale; thousands held by RSF (Al Jazeera Apr 27) extends detention-pattern documentation.
floor_status.exit_criteria: Floor exit not in trajectory. Would require: (a) verifiable cessation of hostilities by both belligerent parties; (b) restoration of humanitarian-access corridors with UN verification; (c) institutional accountability mechanism for documented genocidal acts.

negativeSignals: UN-confirmed genocidal campaign (El-Fasher); 6,000+ documented killings in 3 days; thousands held by RSF; 33.7M in need; 28.9M food insecure; 65%+ hospitals closed; appeal 40% funded.
positiveSignals: None identified at state level. International humanitarian operations continue at funded scale; OHCHR fact-finding access maintained; Sudan Doctors Network continues local documentation.
sector_context: Sudan is the highest-evidence-density floor entity in the countries index. Floor cluster: Sudan, South Sudan, Myanmar, Israel as currently designated. February 2026 OHCHR genocide finding is the most consequential institutional framing event since the floor designation.
open_actions_resolved: El-Fasher Feb 2026 UN genocide finding documented as new institutional framing
RSF detention pattern (Apr 27) added to evidence base
open_actions_carried: Floor exit criteria: not in trajectory
Watch: ICC / international tribunal jurisdiction movement on El-Fasher genocide finding
